algorithms.title: Post-Cardiac Arrest Care Checklist
algorithms.description: Systematic checklist for managing a pediatric patient after return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC), covering airway, oxygenation, ventilation, hemodynamics, temperature, neurological monitoring, and family support.
steps: Step 1 — Confirm and document sustained ROSC with pulse and measurable blood pressure.
Step 2 — Secure the airway: intubate if not already done; continue mechanical ventilation if needed.
Step 3 — Oxygenation: titrate FiO2 to maintain SpO2 94–99%. Avoid both hypoxia and hyperoxia.
Step 4 — Ventilation: target PaCO2 35–45 mmHg (normocapnia). Avoid hyperventilation and hypoventilation.
Step 5 — Obtain 12-lead ECG and portable chest X-ray to evaluate cardiac rhythm and pulmonary status.
Step 6 — Establish continuous monitoring: continuous ECG, pulse oximetry, and arterial blood pressure (arterial line preferred).
Step 7 — Initiate targeted temperature management (TTM) within hours of ROSC: 32–34°C for 48 h then 36–37.5°C, OR 36–37.5°C throughout for 5 days.
Step 8 — Hemodynamic support: maintain SBP above 5th percentile for age. Start vasoactive infusion (epinephrine, norepinephrine, or dopamine) if needed.
Step 9 — Blood glucose: check immediately and maintain normal range. Treat hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia.
Step 10 — Obtain labs: arterial blood gas, lactate, electrolytes, glucose, troponin, CBC.
Step 11 — Echocardiography: assess cardiac function and guide vasoactive medication choices.
Step 12 — Neurological monitoring: continuous EEG if available, especially in comatose children.
Step 13 — Seizure management: treat clinical and subclinical seizures immediately with anticonvulsants.
Step 14 — Identify and treat reversible causes: cardiac, toxicologic, metabolic, and infectious etiologies.
Step 15 — Neuroprognostication: multimodal approach (clinical exam, EEG, biomarkers, MRI, evoked potentials), no earlier than 72 hours post-ROSC.
Step 16 — Family support: designate a support person, communicate openly and compassionately, offer social work and chaplaincy resources.
